ground_truth.title: From 'One Battle After Another' to 'Knives Out,' 10 movies to stream now
ground_truth.author: Brian Truitt
ground_truth.main_content: Need a new movie to watch? Stream these 10 at home for free with Netflix, Disney+, Hulu, HBO Max, Paramount+ and Amazon Prime Video. Football fans and awards-season mavens are feasting this time of year, and there's something for each coming home for a very streaming Christmas. Oscar frontrunner 'One Battle After Another,' starring Leonardo DiCaprio, and 'Him,' with Marlon Wayans as the most unhinged quarterback ever, are among the new movies hitting your favorite services. Netflix, Peacock, Hulu, Disney+ and Amazon's Prime Video have flicks for every taste, from a Riz Ahmed paranoia thriller to a queer zombie flick from director Tina Romero. Here are 10 new and notable movies you can stream right now: 'F1: The Movie' - Brad Pitt brings plenty of personality as an aging driver-for-hire on an underdog racing team. 'Griffin in Summer' - The coming-of-age comedy stars Everett Blunck as high-maintenance creative kid Griffin. 'Him' - The football horror flick stars Tyriq Withers as a pro prospect quarterback. 'Merv' - In the holiday rom-com, Anna (Zooey Deschanel) and Russ (Charlie Cox) are a recently broken-up Boston couple who share their beloved dog. 'One Battle After Another' - Boasting a leading nine Golden Globe nominations, Leonardo DiCaprio stars as a scruffy former revolutionary. 'Relay' - Riz Ahmed stars as a secretive middleman for whistleblowers. 'Roofman' - Channing Tatum's equal parts heartwarming and hilarious performance boosts this stranger-than-fiction, true-life dramedy. 'Spinal Tap II: The End Continues' - Christopher Guest, Michael McKean and Harry Shearer return as the adorably dim, aging rockers. 'Taylor Swift | The Eras Tour | The Final Show' - The concert film captures the global superstar's lively final show filmed in Vancouver. 'Wake Up Dead Man: A Knives Out Mystery' - Rian Johnson's third all-star mystery gives Benoit Blanc his own Watson.
with: Need a new movie to watch? Stream these 10 at home for free with Netflix, Disney+, Hulu, HBO Max, Paramount+ and Amazon Prime Video.
Oscar frontrunner 'One Battle After Another,' starring Leonardo DiCaprio, and 'Him,' with Marlon Wayans as the most unhinged quarterback ever, are among the new movies hitting your favorite services.
Netflix, Peacock, Hulu, Disney+ and Amazon's Prime Video have flicks for every taste, from a Riz Ahmed paranoia thriller to a queer zombie flick from director Tina Romero.
Boasting a leading nine Golden Globe nominations, director Paul Thomas Anderson's timely action dramedy is a must-see heading into Oscar season.
Rian Johnson's third all-star 'Knives Out' mystery gives Benoit Blanc, Daniel Craig's Southern-fried Sherlock, his own Watson.
